members.name: Binesh
members.quote: Staffing is just listening, written down twice.
members.bio: Binesh started TSH in 2020 with a thesis the industry mostly forgot: that placing engineers is a craft, not a numbers game. He runs founder, HR, and admin out of the same notebook — and somehow keeps all three honest. Before TSH, he spent a decade inside the staffing machine and watched what speed-at-any-cost did to candidates. He left to build the slower, quieter version. The one we're still building.

members.name: Shovit
members.quote: The right submission beats ten clever ones.
members.bio: Shovit leads sourcing across our QA and Java desks, and most of the long-running vendor relationships are his. He's the person who notices when a JD doesn't quite match the team behind it — and asks before submitting.

members.name: Hardik
members.quote: Read the role twice. Then read the team.
members.bio: Hardik runs candidate outreach with a patience that the rest of us occasionally borrow. He treats first calls like first impressions — because they are — and writes notes most recruiters wouldn't bother to take.

members.name: Anurodh
members.quote: A good match looks boring on paper. That's the tell.
members.bio: Anurodh focuses on mid-to-senior placements, mostly Java backend. He's quietly relentless about pipeline hygiene and has an unsettling memory for which client wanted what, three quarters ago.

members.name: Suvani
members.quote: Honest representation is just paperwork done with a conscience.
members.bio: Suvani handles candidate representation end-to-end — submission, prep, follow-through. If a rate doesn't add up or a JD is doing too much, she's the one who flags it before anything goes out the door.

members.name: Nikesh
members.quote: An interview is a conversation. We just rehearse the awkward parts.
members.bio: Nikesh runs mock interviews for our QA candidates — automation, frameworks, the whole stack. He's an engineer first, which is the entire point: real engineers helping real engineers prepare for real rooms.

members.name: Akash
members.quote: If you can explain it slowly, you understand it well.
members.bio: Akash sits with Java candidates before their loops — Spring, microservices, JVM, the unglamorous corners. He has a knack for the question behind the question, which is usually the one that gets asked.

members.name: Vivek
members.bio: Vivek splits time between QA and platform interview prep, and runs the system-design walkthroughs for our senior candidates. He'll tell you when an answer is fine — and when it's only fine — which is exactly what you want before a panel.
